snp1.10:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying at Āḷavī in the haunt of the Āḷavaka yakkha.
snp1.10:1.3: Then the Āḷavaka yakkha went to the Blessed One and on arrival said to him:
snp1.10:1.5: [Saying,] “All right, my friend,” the Blessed One went out.
snp1.10:1.7:
“All right, my friend,” the Blessed One went in.
A second time.…
snp1.10:2.2: A third time, the Āḷavaka yakkha said to the Blessed One,
snp1.10:2.4: “All right, my friend,” the Blessed One went out.
snp1.10:2.6: “All right, my friend,” the Blessed One went in.
snp1.10:3.1: Then a fourth time, the Āḷavaka yakkha said to the Blessed One,
snp1.10:3.4: Do what you have to do.”
snp1.10:4.1: “I will ask you a question, contemplative.
snp1.10:4.2:
If you can’t answer me, I will hurl out your mind or rip open your heart or,
grabbing you by the feet, hurl you across the Ganges.”
snp1.10:5.1:
“My friend, I see no one in the cosmos with its devas, Māras,
& Brahmās, in this generation with its contemplatives & brahmans,
its royalty & commonfolk, who could hurl out my mind or rip open my heart or,
grabbing me by the feet, hurl me across the Ganges.
snp1.10:5.2: But nevertheless, ask me what you wish.”
snp1.10:6.1: What is a person’s highest wealth here?
snp1.10:6.3: What is the most excellent of savors?
snp1.10:6.4:
Living in what way is one’s life called the best?
The Buddha:
snp1.10:7.1: Conviction is a person’s highest wealth here.
snp1.10:7.3: Truth is the most excellent of savors.
snp1.10:7.4:
Living with discernment, one’s life is called best.
Āḷavaka:
snp1.10:8.1: How does one cross over the flood?
snp1.10:8.3: How does one overcome suffering & stress?
snp1.10:8.4:
How is a person purified?
The Buddha:
snp1.10:9.1: Through conviction one crosses over the flood.
snp1.10:9.3: Through persistence one overcomes suffering & stress.
snp1.10:9.4:
Through discernment a person is purified.
Āḷavaka:
snp1.10:10.5: Passing from this world to the next world,
snp1.10:10.6:
how does one not grieve?
The Buddha:
snp1.10:14.4:
truth, self-control, stamina, & generosity here can be found.
Āḷavaka:
snp1.10:16.1: It was truly for my well-being
snp1.10:16.2: that the Awakened One came to stay in Āḷavī.
snp1.10:16.4: where what is given bears great fruit.
snp1.10:17.1: I will wander from village to village,
snp1.10:17.3: paying homage to the One Self-Awakened
